dailyJudgementSnippet: Anthropic won a comparatively better day because Claude Code drew real hands-on enthusiasm, practical feature discussion, and a direct claim that it leads on CLI workflows, even though another thread questioned how much supervision Claude-generated code still requires [E3][E4].
winnerExplanation: Anthropic edged the field by getting the strongest favorable product chatter on the front page: one Claude Code thread was plainly driven by daily use and appreciation for the tool's breadth, plus a direct favorable comparison on CLI quality [E3]. A second Claude Code discussion added skepticism about review burden and true productivity gains [E4], but that still left Anthropic better positioned than OpenAI's clearer checkout and Codex criticisms [E1][E2] and Microsoft Copilot's outage-linked complaints [E5].

openai.judgementSnippet: ChatGPT checkout was criticized as worse than normal e-commerce and prone to late out-of-stock surprises, and Codex also took a direct hit when Claude Code users described it as behind on CLI workflows [E1][E2].

anthropic.judgementSnippet: Claude Code got concrete praise from daily users and feature-focused discussion, including a direct comment that it is ahead on the CLI, but another front-page thread pushed back that Claude still needs heavy guidance and extensive review before code is mergeable [E3][E4].

microsoft_copilot.judgementSnippet: The only meaningful Copilot signal came inside GitHub outage complaints, where commenters said GitHub Copilot goes down frequently and argued GitHub is prioritizing AI features over core platform reliability [E5].

evidence.summary: The title says Walmart's ChatGPT checkout converted 3x worse than the website, and top comments argued chat shopping adds friction, clashes with optimized checkout flows, and can surface stock problems too late.
In the Claude Code cheat-sheet thread, a top comment said Claude Code was 'far ahead' of Codex on the CLI front, creating a direct unfavorable comparison for OpenAI's coding product.
The Claude Code cheat-sheet thread featured daily-use testimony, appreciation for how many features exist, and a direct claim that Claude Code is ahead on CLI workflows.

The 'productive with Claude Code' thread drew repeated skepticism that more PRs means better output, and one commenter said Claude needs heavy guidance and multiple feedback rounds before code becomes mergeable.
In a GitHub availability thread, commenters singled out GitHub Copilot as going down often and complained that GitHub is pushing AI features while core reliability and security problems persist.
